author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter VI. 60–72.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have just heard out of the Gospel the words of the Lord which follow the former discourse.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From these a discourse is due to your ears and minds, and it is not unseasonable to-day; for it is concerning the body of the Lord which He said that He gave to be eaten for eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He explained the mode of this bestowal and gift of His, in what manner He gave His flesh to eat, saying, “He that eateth my flesh, and drinketh my blood, dwelleth in me, and I in him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The proof that a man has eaten and drank is this, if he abides and is abode in, if he dwells and is dwelt in, if he adheres so as not to be deserted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, then, He has taught us, and admonished us in mystical words that we may be in His body, in His members under Himself as head, eating His flesh, not abandoning our unity with Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But most of those who were present, by not understanding Him, were offended; for in hearing these things, they thought only of flesh, that which themselves were.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the apostle says, and says what is true, “To be carnally-minded is death.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord gives us His flesh to eat, and yet to understand it according to the flesh is death; while yet He says of His flesh, that therein is eternal life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore we ought not to understand the flesh carnally.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As in these words that follow:

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Many therefore,” not of His enemies, but “of His disciples, when they had heard this, said, This is a hard saying; who can hear it?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If His disciples accounted this saying hard, what must His enemies have thought?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet so it behoved that to be said which should not be understood by all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The secret of God ought to make men eagerly attentive, not hostile.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But these men quickly departed from Him, while the Lord said such things: they did not believe Him to be saying something great, and covering some grace by these words; they understood just according to their wishes, and in the manner of men, that Jesus was able, or was determined upon this, namely, to distribute the flesh with which the Word was clothed, piecemeal, as it were, to those that believe on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “This,” say they, “is a hard saying; who can hear it?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But Jesus, knowing in Himself that His disciples murmured at it,”—for they so said these things with themselves that they might not be heard by Him: but He who knew them in themselves, hearing within Himself,—answered and said, “This offends you;” because I said, I give you my flesh to eat, and my blood to drink, this forsooth offends you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Then what if ye shall see the Son of man ascending where He was before?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did He hereby solve the question that perplexed them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did He hereby uncover the source of their offense?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did clearly, if only they understood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they supposed that He was going to deal out His body to them; but He said that He was to ascend into heaven, of course, whole: “When ye shall see the Son of man ascending where He was before;” certainly then, at least, you will see that not in the manner you suppose does He dispense His body; certainly then, at least, you will understand that His grace is not consumed by tooth-biting.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And He said, “It is the Spirit that quickeneth; the flesh profiteth nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Before we expound this, as the Lord grants us, that other must not be negligently passed over, where He says, “Then what if ye shall see the Son of man ascending where He was before?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ is the Son of man, of the Virgin Mary.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore Son of man He began to be here on earth, where He took flesh from the earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For which cause it was said prophetically, “Truth is sprung from the earth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then what does He mean when He says, “When ye shall see the Son of man ascending where He was before”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there had been no question if He had spoken thus: “If ye shall see the Son of God ascending where He was before.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But since He said, “The Son of man ascending where He was before,” surely the Son of man was not in heaven before the time when He began to have a being on earth?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here, indeed, He said, “where He was before,” just as if He were not there at this time when He spoke these words.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in another place He says, “No man has ascended into heaven but He that came down from heaven, the Son of man who is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said not “was,” but, saith He, “the Son of man who is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was speaking on earth, and He declared Himself to be in heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet He did not speak thus: “No man hath ascended into heaven but He that came down from heaven,” the Son of God, “who is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whither tends it, but to make us understand that which even in the former discourse I commended to your minds, my beloved, that Christ, both God and man, is one person, not two persons, lest our faith be not a trinity, but a quaternity?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Christ, therefore, is one; the Word, soul and flesh, one Christ; the Son of God and Son of man, one Christ; Son of God always, Son of man in time, yet one Christ in regard to unity of person.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In heaven He was when He spoke on earth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was Son of man in heaven in that manner in which He was Son of God on earth; Son of God on earth in the flesh which He took, Son of man in heaven in the unity of person.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is it, then, that He adds?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “It is the Spirit that quickeneth; the flesh profiteth nothing.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us say to Him (for He permits us, not contradicting Him, but desiring to know), O Lord, good Master, in what way does the flesh profit nothing, whilst Thou hast said, “Except a man eat my flesh, and drink my blood, he shall not have life in him?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or does life profit nothing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And why are we what we are, but that we may have eternal life, which Thou dost promise by Thy flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then what means “the flesh profiteth nothing”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It profiteth nothing, but only in the manner in which they understood it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They indeed understood the flesh, just as when cut to pieces in a carcass, or sold in the shambles; not as when it is quickened by the Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore it is said that “the flesh profiteth nothing,” in the same manner as it is said that “knowledge puffeth up.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, ought we at once to hate knowledge?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Far from it!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what means “Knowledge puffeth up”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Knowledge alone, without charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore he added, “but charity edifieth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore add thou to knowledge charity, and knowledge will be profitable, not by itself, but through charity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So also here, “the flesh profiteth nothing,” only when alone.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let the Spirit be added to the flesh, as charity is added to knowledge, and it profiteth very much.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if the flesh profited nothing, the Word would not be made flesh to dwell among us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If through the flesh Christ has greatly profited us, does the flesh profit nothing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it is by the flesh that the Spirit has done somewhat for our salvation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Flesh was a vessel; consider what it held, not what it was.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostles were sent forth; did their flesh profit us nothing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If the apostles’ flesh profited us, could it be that the Lord’s flesh should have profited us nothing?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For how should the sound of the Word come to us except by the voice of the flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence should writing come to us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All these are operations of the flesh, but only when the spirit moves it, as if it were its organ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “it is the Spirit that quickeneth; the flesh profiteth nothing,” as they understood the flesh, but not so do I give my flesh to be eaten.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence “the words,” saith He, “which I have spoken to you are Spirit and life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we have said, brethren, that this is what the Lord had taught us by the eating of His flesh and drinking of His blood, that we should abide in Him and He in us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we abide in Him when we are His members, and He abides in us when we are His temple.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that we may be His members, unity joins us together.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what but love can effect that unity should join us together?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the love of God, whence is it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ask the apostle: “The love of God,” saith he, “is shed abroad in our hearts by the Holy Spirit which is given to us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “it is the Spirit that quickeneth,” for it is the Spirit that makes living members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor does the Spirit make any members to be living except such as it finds in the body, which also the Spirit itself quickens.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Spirit which is in thee, O man, by which it consists that thou art a man, does it quicken a member which it finds separated from thy flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I call thy soul thy spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thy soul quickeneth only the members which are in thy flesh; if thou takest one away, it is no longer quickened by thy soul, because it is not joined to the unity of thy body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These things are said to make us love unity and fear separation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there is nothing that a Christian ought to dread so much as to be separated from Christ’s body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if he is separated from Christ’s body, he is not a member of Christ; if he is not a member of Christ, he is not quickened by the Spirit of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But if any man,” saith the apostle, “have not the Spirit of Christ, he is none of His.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “It is the Spirit,” then, “that quickeneth; the flesh profiteth nothing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The words that I have spoken to you are spirit and life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means “are spirit and life”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They are to be understood spiritually.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou understood spiritually?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “They are spirit and life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou understood carnally?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So also “are they spirit and life,” but are not so to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But,” saith He, “there are some among you that believe not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said not, There are some among you that understand not; but He told the cause why they understand not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “There are some among you that believe not,” and therefore they understand not, because they believe not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the prophet has said, “If ye believe not, ye shall not understand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are united by faith, quickened by understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us first adhere to Him through faith, that there may be that which may be quickened by understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he who adheres not resists; he that resists believes not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how can he that resists be quickened?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is an adversary to the ray of light by which he should be penetrated: he turns not away his eye, but shuts his mind.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “There are,” then, “some who believe not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them believe and open, let them open and be illumined.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were that believed, and who should betray Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Judas also was there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Some indeed, were offended; but he remained to watch his opportunity, not to understand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And because he remained for that purpose, the Lord kept not silence concerning him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He described him not by name, but neither was He silent about him; that all might fear though only one should perish.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But after He spoke, and distinguished those that believe from those that believe not, He clearly showed the cause why they believed not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Therefore I said unto you,” saith He, “that no man can come unto me except it were given to him of my Father.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence to believe is also given to us; for certainly to believe is something.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if it is something great, rejoice that thou hast believed, yet be not lifted up; for “What hast thou that thou didst not receive?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “From that time many of His disciples went back, and walked no more with Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Went back, but after Satan, not after Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For our Lord Christ once addressed Peter as Satan, rather because he wished to precede his Lord, and to give counsel that He should not die, He who had come to die, that we might not die for ever; and He says to him, “Get thee behind me, Satan; for thou savorest not the things that be of God, but the things that be of men.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not drive him back to go after Satan, and so called him Satan; but He made him go behind Himself, that by walking after his Lord he should not be a Satan.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But these went back in the same manner as the apostle says of certain women: “For some are turned back after Satan.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They walked not further with Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, cut off from the body, for perhaps they were not in the body, they have lost life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They must be reckoned among the unbelieving, notwithstanding they were called disciples.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not a few, but “many went back.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This happened, it may be, for our consolation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For sometimes it happens that a man may declare the truth, and that what he says may not be understood, and so they that hear it are offended and go away.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now the man regrets that he had spoken that truth, and he says to himself, “I ought not to have spoken so, I ought not to have said this.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold; it happened to the Lord: He spoke, and lost many; He remained with few.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But yet He was not troubled, because He knew from the beginning who they were that believed and that believed not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If it happen to us, we are sorely perplexed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us find comfort in the Lord, and yet let us speak words with prudence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And now addressing the few that remained: “Then said Jesus to the twelve” (namely, those twelve who remained), “Will ye also,” said He, “go away?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not even Judas departed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But it was already manifest to the Lord why he remained: to us he was made manifest afterwards.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peter answered in behalf of all, one for many, unity for the collective whole: “Then Simon Peter answered Him, Lord, to whom shall we go?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou drivest us from Thee; give us Thy other self.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “To whom shall we go?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we abandon Thee, to whom shall we go?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Thou hast the words of eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See how Peter, by the gift of God and the renewal of the Holy Spirit, understood Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How other than because he believed?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Thou hast the words of eternal life.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Thou hast eternal life in the ministration of Thy body and blood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And we have believed and have known.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not have known and believed, but “believed and known.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we believed in order to know; for if we wanted to know first, and then to believe, we should not be able either to know or to believe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What have we believed and known?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That Thou art Christ, the Son of God;” that is, that Thou art that very eternal life, and that Thou givest in Thy flesh and blood only that which Thou art.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then said the Lord Jesus: “Have not I chosen you twelve, and one of you is a devil?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, should He have said, “I have chosen eleven:” or is a devil also chosen, and among the elect?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Persons are wont to be called “elect” by way of praise: or was man elected because some great good was done by him, without his will and knowledge?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This belongs peculiarly to God; the contrary is characteristic of the wicked.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as wicked men make a bad use of the good works of God; so, on the contrary, God makes a good use of the evil works of wicked men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How good it is that the members of the body are, as they can be disposed only by God, their author and framer!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless what evil use doth wantonness make of the eyes?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What ill use doth falsehood make of the tongue?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Does not the false witness first both slay his own soul with his tongue, and then, after he has destroyed himself, endeavor to injure another?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He makes an ill use of the tongue, but the tongue is not therefore an evil thing; the tongue is God’s work, but iniquity makes an ill use of that good work of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How do they use their feet who run into crimes?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How do murderers employ their hands?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what ill use do wicked men make of those good creatures of God that lie outside of them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With gold they corrupt judgment and oppress the innocent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Bad men make a bad use of the very light; for by evil living they employ even the very light with which they see into the service of their villanies.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A bad man, when going to do a bad deed, wishes the light to shine for him, lest he stumble; he who has already stumbled and fallen within; that which he is afraid of in his body has already befallen him in his heart.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hence, to avoid the tediousness of running through them separately, a bad man makes a bad use of all the good creatures of God: a good man, on the contrary, makes a good use of the evil deeds of wicked men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what is so good as the one God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Since, indeed, the Lord Himself said, “There is none good, but the one God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By how much He is better, then, by so much the better use He makes of our evil deeds.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What worse than Judas?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Among all that adhered to the Master, among the twelve, to him was committed the common purse; to him was allotted the dispensing for the poor.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Unthankful for so great a favor, so great an honor, he took the money, and lost righteousness: being dead, he betrayed life: Him whom he followed as a disciple, he persecuted as an enemy.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All this evil was Judas’s; but the Lord employed his evil for good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He endured to be betrayed, to redeem us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, Judas’s evil was turned to good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How many martyrs has Satan persecuted!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If Satan left off persecuting, we should not to-day be celebrating the very glorious crown of Saint Laurence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If then God employs the evil works of the devil himself for good, what the bad man effects, by making a bad use, is to hurt himself, not to contradict the goodness of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Master makes use of that man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if He knew not how to make use of him, the Master contriver would not have permitted him to be.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, He saith, “One of you is a devil,” whilst I have chosen you twelve.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This saying, “I have chosen you twelve,” may be understood in this way, that twelve is a sacred number.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the honor of that number was not taken away because one was lost, for another was chosen into the place of the one that perished.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The number remained a sacred number, a number containing twelve: because they were to make known the Trinity throughout the whole world, that is, throughout the four quarters of the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That is the reason of the three times four.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Judas, then only cut himself off, not profaned the number twelve: he abandoned his Teacher, for God appointed a successor to take his place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All this that the Lord spoke concerning His flesh and blood;—and in the grace of that distribution He promised us eternal life, and that He meant those that eat His flesh and drink His blood to be understood, from the fact of their abiding in Him and He in them; and that they understood not who believed not; and that they were offended through their understanding spiritual things in a carnal sense; and that, while these were offended and perished, the Lord was present for the consolation of the disciples who remained, for proving whom He asked, “Will ye also go away?” that the reply of their steadfastness might be known to us, for He knew that they remained with Him;—let all this, then, avail us to this end, most beloved, that we eat not the flesh and blood of Christ merely in the sacrament, as many evil men do, but that we eat and drink to the participation of the Spirit, that we abide as members in the Lord’s body, to be quickened by His Spirit, and that we be not offended, even if many do now with us eat and drink the sacraments in a temporal manner, who shall in the end have eternal torments.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For at present Christ’s body is as it were mixed on the threshing-floor: “But the Lord knoweth them that are His.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou knowest what thou threshest, that the substance is there hidden, that the threshing has not consumed what the winnowing has purged; certain are we, brethren, that all of us who are in the Lord’s body, and abide in Him, that He also may abide in us, have of necessity to live among evil men in this world even unto the end.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I do not say among those evil men who blaspheme Christ; for there are now few found who blaspheme with the tongue, but many who do so by their life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Among those, then, we must necessarily live even unto the end.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what is this that He saith: “He that abideth in me, and I in him”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, but that which the martyrs heard: “He that persevereth unto the end, the same shall be saved”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How did Saint Laurence, whose feast we celebrate to-day, abide in Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He abode even to temptation, abode even to tyrannical questioning, abode even to bitterest threatening, abode even to destruction;—that were a trifle, abode even to savage torture.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he was not put to death quickly, but tormented in the fire: he was allowed to live a long time; nay, not allowed to live a long time, but forced to die a slow, lingering death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then, in that lingering death, in those torments, because he had well eaten and well drunk, as one who had feasted on that meat, as one intoxicated with that cup, he felt not the torments.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He was there who said, “It is the Spirit that quickeneth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the flesh indeed was burning, but the Spirit was quickening the soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He shrunk not back, and he mounted into the kingdom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the holy martyr Xystus, whose day we celebrated five days ago, had said to him, “Mourn not, my son;” for Xystus was a bishop, he was a deacon.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Mourn not,” said he; “thou shall follow me after three days.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He said three days, meaning the interval between the day of Saint Xystus’s suffering and that of Saint Laurence’s suffering, which falls on to-day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Three days is the interval.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What comfort!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He says not, “Mourn not, my son; the persecution will cease, and thou wilt be safe;” but, “do not mourn: whither I precede thou shalt follow; nor shall thy pursuit be deferred: three days will be the interval, and thou shalt be with me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He accepted the oracle, vanquished the devil, and attained to the triumph.
